compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, I can run /dso:sprint on a bug and have it investigated properly
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Route bug-typed primary tickets through /dso:fix-bug as a sub-skill, ensuring hypothesis validation and RED-before-fix gates apply regardless of clarity score.
**Why**: Bugs require investigation methodology that sprint's generic task-execution path does not provide. This prevents practitioners from accidentally skipping proper investigation by running sprint on a bug.
**Scope**:
- IN: Bug-typed primary tickets dispatched to /dso:fix-bug as sub-skill passing primary_ticket_id; fix-bug investigation gates (hypothesis validation, RED-before-fix) enforced
- OUT: Non-bug ticket routing (Story 3), brainstorm escalation path (deferred to dependency epic ac09-05b7)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, bug-typed primary tickets that pass the clarity gate are dispatched to /dso:fix-bug as a sub-skill, with primary_ticket_id passed as the ticket identifier
  ← Satisfies: "Bug-typed primary tickets that pass the clarity gate are still dispatched to /dso:fix-bug as a sub-skill"
- When this story is complete, bug-typed primary tickets that fail the clarity gate are offered /dso:fix-bug as an escalation option
  ← Satisfies: "User escalation presents options: investigate via /dso:fix-bug (bugs only)"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- None — Story 3 handles the routing infrastructure; this story adds bug-specific dispatch logic on top
